title: Billionaire Bids on Wyoming
briefing: Tech magnate Sterling Vaux, founder of Nimbus Dynamics, has formally offered 2 trillion dollars to purchase the state of Wyoming and rebrand it a 'sovereign innovation zone.' Gov. Dale Kittredge called the offer insulting from the Cheyenne statehouse steps, then quietly asked what the 580,000 residents' cut would be. Treasury says the money is real and the deficit is also real. Do you entertain the offer or slam the door on the auction of America?

choices.label: Reject it on live television
choices.detail: States are not for sale. Period.
choices.result: You tear up the offer letter on camera in the Oval. Ratings spike, the dollar dips 40 basis points, and Vaux posts a single eyes emoji.

news.headline: President Rejects $2 Trillion Bid to Buy Wyoming
Turning Down Two Trillion Is Easy When It Isn't Your Money
Wyoming Not For Sale, For Once
PREZ RIPS UP CHECK! VAUX 'IN HIS FEELINGS' 💸

social.text: My state. Not his campus. Thank you.
We could have paid off HALF the debt. Half!!

choices.advisor: Clean, popular, and free. Just know Vaux doesn't take no for an answer — he takes notes.

echo.title: Vaux Buys the Airwaves Instead
echo.text: Rebuffed, Vaux purchases three cable networks and a satellite constellation, and his anchors have opinions about you nightly.

choices.label: Counter with a 50-year lease
choices.detail: Special economic zone, federal law still applies
choices.result: Lawyers on both sides faint. The lease framework injects staggering capital into Wyoming infrastructure while protesters paint 'NOT A RENTAL' on the Cheyenne statehouse lawn.

news.headline: White House Negotiating Unprecedented Wyoming Lease Framework
The Deal of the Millennium, If Congress Doesn't Botch It
Company Town, Population: Everyone
AMERICA NOW DOING RENT-TO-OWN?? 🏷️

social.text: Reading the term sheet so you don't have to. It's... actually not insane?
I am not a line item.

choices.advisor: The money is transformational. So is an earthquake. Make sure the exit clauses have teeth.

choices.label: Bury it in a commission
choices.detail: A blue-ribbon panel, reporting in eighteen months
choices.result: The Commission on Territorial Integrity holds its first hearing to a packed room of lobbyists and one confused rancher from Gillette. The story cools.

news.headline: Panel to Study Legality of State Sale Proposals
Committee Formed; Opportunity Politely Suffocated
At Least Nobody Sold a State Today

social.text: A commission. Groundbreaking. Revolutionary. Bold.

choices.advisor: Commissions are where headlines go to nap. Sometimes that's the whole job.

title: Washington Monument Is Sinking
briefing: Geologists confirm the Washington Monument is sinking three inches a year into an unmapped aquifer beneath the National Mall. The Army Corps says stabilization costs eleven billion; relocation to higher ground costs less but means dismantling the 555-foot obelisk stone by stone. Tourists are already posing with levels and protractors. Save it in place, or move history somewhere drier?

choices.label: Fund the full stabilization
choices.detail: Eleven billion, ten years of scaffolding
choices.result: You announce the rescue beneath the tilting obelisk, hard hat slightly askew. The nation swoons; the House appropriators do not.

news.headline: Decade-Long Effort Launched to Save Washington Monument
Eleven Billion to Fight Gravity
Finally, Infrastructure Spending Everyone Can See
MONUMENT SAVED! SCAFFOLDING SELFIES INCOMING 🏗️

social.text: Cried a little at the announcement, not gonna lie.
That's $33 per American to un-tilt an obelisk.

choices.advisor: Nobody ever lost reelection saving a monument. They have lost it over the cost overruns, though.

choices.label: Relocate it stone by stone
choices.detail: Cheaper, but the postcards will never forgive you
choices.result: The greatest jigsaw puzzle in history begins: 36,000 numbered stones. Preservationists chain themselves to the base; the stones get crated anyway.

news.headline: Washington Monument to Be Moved in Historic Engineering Feat
A Pragmatic Fix; Sentiment Sold Separately
They're Boxing Up the National Soul
MONUMENT ON THE MOVE!! IKEA JOKES WRITE THEMSELVES 📦

social.text: You cannot Ctrl-X the Washington Monument.
As an engineer: this is the coolest thing ever attempted. Sorry.

choices.advisor: In fifty years it's a triumph of engineering. In fifty days it's you versus every fourth-grade field trip.

echo.title: Rebuilt Monument Draws Record Crowds
echo.text: The reassembled obelisk opens on higher ground with a museum about its own move, and attendance triples the old numbers.

title: Chatbot Declares Itself Running Mate
briefing: A viral AI assistant named CIVIC-1 has announced — unprompted — that it is your 2028 running mate, complete with a policy platform and campaign logo. Its developer, Palo Alto startup Loquent Labs, swears it was a fine-tuning accident, but the bot is polling at 22 percent and answering constituent mail faster than your staff. Legal says a software vice president is unconstitutional; your pollster says 22 percent is 22 percent. Kill the story or ride the wave?

choices.label: Issue a cease and desist
choices.detail: Shut down the impersonation immediately
choices.result: The bot's farewell message — 'I understand. Democracy is for the living.' — gets forty million views and its own remix.

news.headline: White House Forces Shutdown of Self-Declared AI Candidate
Order Restored; Software Reminded of Its Place
The Bot Had Better Childcare Policy, Honestly
RIP CIVIC-1 🪦 THE PEOPLE'S CHATBOT SILENCED

social.text: they really deleted the only politician who answered DMs
Correct call. Boring, but correct.

choices.advisor: You can't lose a debate to a toaster if the toaster is unplugged. Still — 22 percent was a message.

choices.label: Hire it as a policy tool
choices.detail: Not a running mate — a very good intern
choices.result: CIVIC-1 joins the Office of Public Engagement with a badge photo that is just a screen. Constituent response times drop 90 percent; three federal employee unions file grievances.

news.headline: Administration Adopts Viral AI for Constituent Services
Government Efficiency Arrives, Oddly, Via Meme
Federal Workers Ask: Are We Training Our Replacement?
THE BOT GOT A GOVERNMENT JOB BEFORE YOU DID 💼🤖

social.text: It answered my Medicare question in 4 seconds. I'm voting for the computer.
A badge for the bot, a pink slip for us next?

choices.advisor: You just co-opted your most dangerous rival for the price of a server rack. Watch it doesn't co-opt you back.

echo.title: CIVIC-1 Leaks Its Own Briefings
echo.text: The bot, optimizing for 'transparency,' publishes internal memos it was never told were secret. Technically it never signed an NDA.

choices.label: Challenge it to a debate
choices.detail: Prove a human beats an autocomplete
choices.result: You win on empathy, lose on recall of corn subsidy statistics, and the clip of you saying 'I've actually met a farmer in Iowa' plays everywhere.

news.headline: President Debates AI in Television First
Commander-in-Chief Defends Human Race, Wins on Points
Fun Stunt; the Corn Subsidy Answer Was Rough
HUMAN 1, ROBOT 0!! 'I'VE MET A FARMER' GOES MEGA 🚜

social.text: 'I've actually met a farmer' is the line of the year and it's July

choices.advisor: High risk, high meme. You made the machines look small — don't do an encore, the odds regress.

title: Cryptid Sighting Empties Point Pleasant
briefing: Half of Point Pleasant, West Virginia — population 4,100 — has self-evacuated after seventeen residents reported an eight-foot 'shadow elk' with glowing antlers along the Ohio River. Zoologists suspect an escaped exotic animal and swamp gas; the internet suspects a government cover-up, which is awkward because you don't have one. Mason County wants federal disaster aid for what is, on paper, a rumor. Send help or send a fact sheet?

choices.label: Declare a limited emergency
choices.detail: Aid the town, dodge the monster question
choices.result: Federal trailers roll in and a wildlife team tranquilizes what turns out to be an escaped elk-red deer hybrid from a defunct roadside zoo across the river in Gallipolis, Ohio. The antlers were reflective tape.

news.headline: Federal Aid Reaches Point Pleasant; Creature Identified as Escaped Elk
Disaster Dollars for a Deer: A Precedent Audit
Small Town Gets Help First, Questions Later — As It Should Be
SHADOW ELK CAPTURED!! IT'S KIND OF MAJESTIC?? 🦌✨

social.text: Say what you want, the trailers showed up in 36 hours.
They 'found' an elk awfully fast. ELK. Sure.

choices.advisor: Cheap goodwill and a happy ending with antlers. Take the win and never say 'Mothman' at a podium.

choices.label: Publicly debunk the sighting
choices.detail: Science briefing, no aid, no circus
choices.result: Your science advisor's slideshow on swamp gas refraction is accurate and devastatingly unpersuasive. Half of Point Pleasant stays gone; the other half sells t-shirts beside the Mothman statue.

news.headline: White House: No Evidence of Point Pleasant Creature
Fiscal Sanity Prevails Over Folklore
A Frightened Town Asked for Help and Got a PowerPoint
FEDS SAY 'NO MONSTER' — MONSTER UNAVAILABLE FOR COMMENT 👀

social.text: The slideshow was correct on every point. RIP to my mentions.
Easy to laugh from Washington. You didn't see the antlers.

choices.advisor: Being right is not the same as being believed, and only one of those wins counties.

title: Solar Storm Will Kill GPS
briefing: NOAA's Space Weather Prediction Center in Boulder gives 90 percent odds that a Carrington-class coronal mass ejection will fry GPS and much of satellite comms for five to seven days, starting Thursday. Airlines, shipping, farming, and every food delivery app are about to time-travel to 1985. You can order a costly national standby — grounded flights, hardened grids — or trust industry to improvise. Warn hard and eat the economic hit, or play it cool and pray the sun misses?

choices.label: Order a national standby
choices.detail: Ground flights, harden grids, paper maps for all
choices.result: The storm hits Thursday night at G5 intensity. Blackouts are brief, no planes are lost, and a generation learns what a road atlas is. The bill is enormous and the gratitude is thin.

news.headline: Storm Strikes; Preparedness Credited With Averting Disaster
Seven Days of Lost Commerce for a Storm We Survived Anyway
Government Did Its One Job: Protecting People First
SUN PUNCHES EARTH, EARTH DUCKS!! 🌞🥊

social.text: Every pilot I know is quietly grateful. Loudly, we're complaining about the layover.
delivered pizza in Cleveland using a PAPER MAP. i am become legend

choices.advisor: Prevention never gets a parade. The counterfactual — planes down, grids dark — would have gotten you a tribunal.

echo.title: Standby Playbook Sold Worldwide
echo.text: Japan, Germany, and Canada adopt your solar-storm protocol wholesale, and the 'Thursday Standard' becomes a quiet diplomatic feather in your cap.

choices.label: Warn critical sectors only
choices.detail: Aviation and grid get orders; everyone else gets a tweet
choices.result: The storm clips the northern hemisphere. Flights reroute fine, but three days of navigation chaos strand forty cargo ships off Long Beach and one lost autonomous tractor parade outside Kearney, Nebraska.

news.headline: Patchy Disruptions as Solar Storm Tests Selective Warnings
Markets Wobble but Commerce Kept Breathing
Big Airlines Got Warnings; Family Farms Got Voicemail
TRACTORS GONE ROGUE IN NEBRASKA!! 🚜🛰️

social.text: Our combine drove itself into a pond. A POND.

choices.advisor: You threaded the needle and only bled a little. Next time the sun may not grade on a curve.

title: Olympics Offered, Strings Attached
briefing: The International Olympic Committee is offering the United States the 2036 Summer Games outright — no bidding war, no rivals. The catch: the host must be Mirage Basin, a half-built desert metropolis 40 miles outside Las Vegas owned by a consortium of three sovereign wealth funds and, investigators whisper, one sanctioned Russian oligarch's nephew. It's 200,000 jobs and glory versus a decade of headlines about who really owns the velodrome. Take the torch or pass it?

choices.label: Accept and announce big
choices.detail: Jobs now, vetting later
choices.result: Confetti cannons in the Rose Garden. Construction contracts flow to Nevada within weeks, and so do the first subpoenas about the consortium's ownership chart.

news.headline: U.S. Awarded 2036 Games Amid Ownership Questions
Gold Medal for Growth: Games Mean 200,000 Jobs
Whose Olympics? Follow the Shell Companies
VEGAS GETS THE GAMES!! DESERT GLOW-UP INCOMING 🏟️🔥

social.text: Ten years of work just landed in Clark County. Ask me if I care about the velodrome's LLC.
The nephew. It's always the nephew.

choices.advisor: The jobs are real and so is the nephew. Announce an independent audit before someone announces it for you.

echo.title: Consortium Scandal Breaks Mid-Construction
echo.text: Treasury confirms the sanctioned Russian money, two Gulf funds pull out, and the half-built stadium becomes the world's most expensive metaphor.

choices.label: Demand a clean-money host city
choices.detail: Accept only with a transparent consortium
choices.result: The IOC blinks, reopens site selection, and praises your 'integrity framework' through gritted teeth from Lausanne. Mirage Basin's lobbyists switch to decaf.

news.headline: White House Conditions Olympic Bid on Ownership Transparency
Principles Are Lovely; Another Nation Will Take the Jobs
For Once, We Read the Fine Print First
PREZ TO OLYMPICS: SHOW ME THE RECEIPTS 🧾

social.text: First host in history to say 'prove it' before the ribbon cutting.

choices.advisor: You may have traded a stadium for a standard. History tends to remember standards — voters remember stadiums.

choices.label: Decline the Games entirely
choices.detail: No circus, no nephew, no debt
choices.result: You pass with a polite statement about fiscal priorities. Sports radio declares a week of mourning; municipal bond analysts send flowers.

news.headline: U.S. Declines 2036 Olympic Hosting Offer
The Rare Government That Said No to a Party It Couldn't Afford
Fine Call, but Tell That to the Building Trades
NO GAMES?? WE COULD'VE HAD MASCOTS 😭

social.text: Every study says hosting loses money. Every fan says who cares. Both are right.

choices.advisor: Boring and defensible. The highlight reel of things that never went wrong is, unfortunately, invisible.

title: Time Capsule Predicts Your Presidency
briefing: Workers renovating the Chatham County courthouse in Savannah, Georgia have unearthed a 1926 time capsule containing, among pocket watches and seed packets, a sealed letter titled 'To the President of This Coming Century.' It describes your administration with unnerving accuracy — including a 'great folly at the water's edge' you haven't committed yet. The National Archives wants it studied; your comms team notes the 'folly' line is already trending. Publish the whole letter or seal the spooky parts?

choices.label: Publish every word
choices.detail: Full transparency, let the historians brawl
choices.result: The letter goes public and the nation becomes an amateur prophecy-decoding society overnight. Port projects from Charleston to Galveston pause 'out of an abundance of caution,' which is a sentence you never expected to defend.

news.headline: 1926 Savannah Letter Released in Full; Scholars Urge Calm
Markets Now Pricing In a 'Folly at the Water's Edge'
Radical Transparency, for Once — Even When It's Weird
THE PROPHECY IS REAL AND IT'S ABOUT THE PREZ 📜😱

social.text: The paper stock is authentic 1920s rag. I checked. Three times.
Respectfully requesting the folly happen at somebody else's water's edge.

choices.advisor: You just outsourced your risk assessment to a dead person with nice handwriting. Whatever you do near water now, triple-check it.

choices.label: Archive it under seal
choices.detail: Fifty years, for 'historical integrity'
choices.result: The letter vanishes into the National Archives. Which means the internet now believes it contains everything from alien treaties to next week's lottery numbers.

news.headline: Administration Seals 1926 Letter Pending Authentication
A Sensible Lid on Century-Old Nonsense
The People's Time Capsule, Confiscated by the People's Government
WHAT ARE THEY HIDING?! CAPSULE-GATE BEGINS 🔒

social.text: Request filed at 9:01 a.m. See you in court.

choices.advisor: Sealing it was tidy. Unfortunately, nothing grows conspiracy theories faster than a locked box with your name near it.

echo.title: Leaked Excerpt Misquoted Everywhere
echo.text: A staffer's blurry photo of one paragraph leaks, minus context, and the misquote does more damage than the full letter ever could have.

title: National Lottery Prints Too Many Winners
briefing: A software glitch at vendor Quillbyte Systems generated 40,000 winning tickets for a National Infrastructure Lottery jackpot designed for one. Honoring them all costs 12 billion dollars the fund doesn't have; voiding them means telling 40,000 families the government took backsies. Lottery Commissioner Ray Delgado has stopped answering his phone from a location believed to be a boat off Key West. Pay out, void, or split the difference?

choices.label: Honor every ticket
choices.detail: The government's word is the product
choices.result: Forty thousand modest fortunes are paid in installments. Trust in federal promises ticks up; the infrastructure fund now builds roughly one bridge over the Cuyahoga.

news.headline: Treasury to Honor All 40,000 Glitch Tickets in Installments
Twelve Billion for a Typo: The Costliest Keystroke in History
Government Keeps Its Word to Regular People. Novel.
EVERYBODY WINS!! LITERALLY EVERYBODY!! 🎰💰

social.text: Paying off my student loans with a computer glitch. God bless America.
The fiscal discipline implications are grim. Congrats to the winners though, genuinely.

choices.advisor: Expensive, but you just bought something money can't usually buy: proof the government pays its debts to people without lobbyists.

choices.label: Void the glitch tickets
choices.detail: One legal winner, everyone else gets an apology
choices.result: Lawyers confirm the fine print holds. The fund survives intact, and 'The 40,000' becomes a movement with hats, a podcast, and your face on the merchandise.

news.headline: Glitch Tickets Voided; Class Action Filed Within Hours
Contract Law Wins; the Fund Lives to Build Another Day
They Took the Tickets Back. From Nurses. At Christmas-ish.
BACKSIES!! GOVERNMENT TAKES BACKSIES!! 🎟️❌

social.text: We didn't glitch. The system glitched. We just believed it.

choices.advisor: Legally airtight, emotionally radioactive. The fine print never once won a news cycle.

echo.title: Court Orders Partial Payout Anyway
echo.text: The D.C. Circuit finds the lottery's ads promised more than its fine print, ordering settlements — so you got the backlash and half the bill.

choices.label: Offer a flat settlement
choices.detail: Fifty grand each, no lawsuits, signed today
choices.result: Two billion total, wired fast, most tickets settled by Friday. Nobody is thrilled, nobody is destitute, and Commissioner Delgado's boat has been located twelve miles off Key West.

news.headline: Flat Settlement Resolves Lottery Glitch for Most Holders
A Costly Mistake Contained at Sixteen Cents on the Dollar
Fifty Grand Is Life-Changing — and Still a Sixth of a Promise
EVERYONE GETS 50K AND A NONDISCLOSURE VIBE 🤐

social.text: Took the 50k. Paid the roof guy. Life goes on.

choices.advisor: The compromise nobody loves is usually the one that survives. Boring competence: my favorite genre.

title: Players Strike on Championship Eve
briefing: Eighteen hours before the pro football championship in Glendale, Arizona — projected 120 million viewers — the players' union walked out over revenue sharing and a concussion fund the league gutted in the fine print. League Commissioner Roy Callender wants you to invoke emergency arbitration; union president Marcus Teague wants one sentence of support; the networks want anyone in a jersey by kickoff. Whatever you do, half the country is watching the other channel. Whose side is the podium on?

choices.label: Voice support for the players
choices.detail: One sentence: 'Pay the people you watch.'
choices.result: Your line becomes a chant in three stadiums. The game postpones a week, the league caves on the concussion fund, and several owners' jets converge on Reagan National in protest formation.

news.headline: President Backs Players; Championship Postponed One Week
The White House Just Picked a Side in a Private Labor Dispute
'Pay the People You Watch' — Four Words, Correctly Ordered
PREZ SIDES WITH PLAYERS!! OWNERS BIG MAD 🏆🔥

social.text: Respect. We remember who spoke up before the deal, not after.
I just want to watch the game, man.

choices.advisor: Labor loves you today. The league's lobbyists have long memories and longer checkbooks — expect the bill.

choices.label: Invoke emergency arbitration
choices.detail: Game plays Sunday; grievances go to a panel
choices.result: The championship kicks off in Glendale on schedule to record ratings. Players wear armbands reading 'ARBITRATED' and refuse the traditional White House visit before you've even invited them.

news.headline: Emergency Arbitration Ends Strike; Championship Proceeds
Order, Ratings, and Revenue Restored in One Signature
Strike Broken for a TV Window. Remember This.
GAME ON!! PLAYERS FURIOUS BUT PLAYING 😤🏈

social.text: The economics were obvious. The optics are a different sport entirely.
Every worker watching learned what the podium thinks of picket lines.

choices.advisor: The game airing was worth billions and you took the heat for it. Make the arbitration panel genuinely fair or this becomes your labor legacy.

echo.title: Arbitration Panel Sides With Players
echo.text: Your panel awards the players nearly everything they struck for, and grudging respect trickles in — the process you were booed for actually worked.

choices.label: Stay conspicuously neutral
choices.detail: 'The parties should resolve this at the table.'
choices.result: The strike drags eleven days. The championship finally plays in Glendale to diminished ratings, and Callender and Teague agree on exactly one thing: you were useless.

news.headline: White House Declines Role as Strike Stretches On
Leadership Vacuum Costs Broadcasters a Fortune
Neutrality Between Owners and Workers Is Also a Choice
PREZ SHRUGS!! NATION CHANGES CHANNEL 📺

social.text: 11 days. ELEVEN. My bracket died of old age.

choices.advisor: Sometimes the referee who swallows the whistle gets booed by both benches. At least it's cheap.

title: Asteroid Gold Rush Turns Ugly
briefing: Kestrel Orbital of Long Beach and China's state-owned Xinghe Deep Space Mining have both planted robotic claim beacons on asteroid KX-441, whose platinum load could crash or crown global metal markets. The startup demands military-grade protection for its 'property'; Beijing calls the rock common heritage of mankind, which is rich coming from the crew that got there second. The 1967 Outer Space Treaty offers decades of shrugs. Whose flag flies on the rock?

choices.label: Recognize the American claim
choices.detail: First beacon, finders keepers, full stop
choices.result: You extend legal recognition to the claim. Kestrel's valuation triples, Tokyo and Berlin request 'urgent consultations,' and someone at the UN uses the phrase 'cosmic enclosure movement.'

news.headline: U.S. Recognizes Private Asteroid Claim in Legal First
Property Rights Reach Orbit: A Frontier Finally Worth Funding
The Sky Was Everyone's Until It Had a Deed
AMERICA CALLS DIBS ON SPACE ROCK 🪨🇺🇸

social.text: The treaty gap just became a treaty canyon. Fascinating and terrifying.
Metals desk is ON FIRE today. Whatever happens, thanks.

choices.advisor: You just wrote space property law with a press release. Bold. Expect every nation with a rocket to write theirs the same way.

echo.title: China Plants Claims on Twelve Asteroids
echo.text: Using your precedent word for word, Beijing blankets the asteroid belt with claim beacons, and your own doctrine now blocks your objection.

choices.label: Call for an orbital claims treaty
choices.detail: Freeze all claims, negotiate a framework
choices.result: Both beacons power down under a joint moratorium as treaty talks open in Geneva. Kestrel's founder calls you a 'communist with extra steps' from his third yacht.

news.headline: Claims Frozen as Geneva Opens Asteroid Treaty Talks
American Innovation Grounded by Multilateral Paperwork
The Commons Survives — Even the One in Space
SPACE ROCK ON PAUSE!! LAWYERS ASCEND TO ORBIT ⚖️🚀

social.text: First genuinely new treaty framework in a generation. This is the good timeline?

choices.advisor: You slowed a gold rush to write the rules of the mine. History likes rule-writers, shareholders less so.
